title: Technical Problem Solving, Coding, and Work-Sample Boundaries · Hiring Signal Forensics
summary: A realistic technical problem solving, coding, and work-sample boundaries artifact contains one plausible targeting, evidence, integrity, access, privacy, process, or decision defect. Locate the first unsupported claim before revising it. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in observable technical interview practice system and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
Externalize assumptions, alternatives, invariants, tradeoffs, checkpoints, uncertainty, and help requests without narrating every keystroke.
Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Problem Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The debug variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.

Inspect the Problem Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Problem Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Problem Contract, keep Problem Contract connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Problem Contract, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Problem Contract, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Problem Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Problem Contract, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The debug variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Problem Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Beginning to code immediately demonstrates seniority.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Problem Contract.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Problem Contract, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

career-sample-boundary-hwllom:

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
